pr.title: Remove DEBUG/NDEBUG flags from build script
pr.author: BR-

summary: Removed automatic DEBUG/NDEBUG preprocessor definitions from configure.py's debug flag handling. The --debug option now only enables Metrowerks symbol output with "-sym on" and no longer adds "-DDEBUG=1"; non-debug builds no longer add "-sym off" or "-DNDEBUG=1" from this block. The PR body says these defines were only used by extern/dolphin/ and caused build failures when using --debug to get line number information in objdiff.
what_changed: In configure.py, the args.debug branch was simplified from extending cflags_base with ["-sym on", "-DDEBUG=1"] to appending only "-sym on".
The non-debug else branch that previously added ["-sym off", "-DNDEBUG=1"] was removed entirely.
The max error flag handling after the debug block was left unchanged.
smart_moves: Separated the need for debug line/symbol information from C preprocessor debug-mode semantics; --debug can now be used for objdiff line numbers without forcing DEBUG-dependent code paths.
Kept the change minimal and localized to configure.py, reducing risk to unrelated build logic.
Avoided globally defining NDEBUG in normal builds when the PR evidence says these macros were only relevant to extern/dolphin/.
decomp_lessons: Build flags used for decompilation tooling convenience, such as symbol or line-number output for objdiff, should not implicitly change source-level preprocessor behavior unless that behavior is required for matching.
Global DEBUG or NDEBUG defines can perturb third-party or extern code and may cause build failures; prefer narrow, file- or subsystem-specific defines when needed.
When a build option is used primarily for analysis tooling, keep it semantically focused so developers can enable diagnostics without altering compilation paths.
naming_conventions: No symbol or function naming conventions were discussed in this PR.
The PR reinforces keeping command-line option semantics clear: args.debug in configure.py should mean debug symbol output here, not automatic DEBUG/NDEBUG macro selection.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Use "-sym on" to emit symbol or line information useful for objdiff without also defining DEBUG=1.
Avoid adding DEBUG/NDEBUG globally during matching work because those macros may alter compiled code paths or external dependency behavior.
The removed "-sym off" in the non-debug path suggests the build script no longer needs to explicitly disable symbols when --debug is absent, based on the shown diff.
review_feedback: No review comments were present in the provided PR slice.
The PR body states the rationale: DEBUG/NDEBUG flags were only used by extern/dolphin/ and caused build failures when using --debug for line number info in objdiff.
follow_up_queries: Check whether extern/dolphin/ still relies on DEBUG or NDEBUG and whether any subsystem-specific defines are needed there.
Audit configure.py for other flags where analysis/debug tooling options may accidentally change compiled code behavior.
Search for remaining global -DDEBUG or -DNDEBUG injection points outside configure.py.
